title: Use the event_loop fixture
body:
Refs:
- https://github.com/simonw/datasette/issues/2461#issuecomment-2634920351

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://datasette--2463.org.readthedocs.build/en/2463/

<!-- readthedocs-preview datasette end -->

step_plan.summary: Update Datasette’s test infrastructure to explicitly use pytest-asyncio’s `event_loop` fixture in key async test fixtures/helpers, improving compatibility with `asyncio_mode = strict` and avoiding reuse of loop-bound resources across tests.

steps.operation: Update the async ds_client fixture to explicitly depend on pytest-asyncio’s event loop and ensure the created client is not reused across closed loops
steps.reason: With `asyncio_mode = strict`, pytest-asyncio is stricter about loop ownership and lifecycle; async resources (http clients, background tasks, app startup) must be created and used on the same loop provided by pytest-asyncio. A module-level cached `_ds_client` can accidentally bind to a loop that later gets closed, causing flakiness and warnings/failures. Adding an `event_loop` fixture dependency (and adjusting caching/scope accordingly) makes loop usage explicit and compatible with newer pytest-asyncio behavior.
steps.side_effects: May increase per-test setup cost if caching is removed or reduced; could require updating tests that assumed a shared client instance; may expose previously-hidden teardown ordering issues (client close/startup/shutdown) that need to be addressed.

steps.operation: Re-evaluate and adjust test collection ordering hook to ensure it remains correct under strict asyncio mode and any fixture/loop changes
steps.reason: This hook likely enforces ordering to avoid certain tests triggering async behavior too early or to sequence integration tests. Changing how the event loop is obtained/used (and possibly changing fixture scopes) can alter when async fixtures are instantiated; collection/execution ordering constraints may need to be updated to avoid depending on implicit loop initialization.
steps.side_effects: Could change test run order, which may reveal hidden inter-test coupling; may affect xdist/parallel runs if ordering assumptions were relied upon; should be validated by running the full test suite.

steps.operation: Ensure wait_until_responds remains robust when used alongside loop-scoped async server startup and strict-mode event loop management
steps.reason: If server startup/readiness checks are executed while async fixtures now explicitly use `event_loop`, readiness polling must not inadvertently rely on a different loop/thread timing or on resources tied to a loop that is not running. Tightening this helper (timeouts/backoff/exception handling) helps prevent flakes when loop creation/teardown becomes more deterministic under strict mode.
steps.side_effects: May slightly change timing characteristics of integration tests (e.g., longer waits before failing); could require adjusting timeouts in CI; should reduce intermittent failures in slow environments.
